description: Total revenue by city last 30 days (orders+users only)
question: What is the total revenue by city in the last 30 days?
plan_objective: Join orders o with users u on o.user_id = u.user_id. Filter o.created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'. Group by u.city. Return u.city and SUM(o.grand_total) as total_revenue ordered DESC.
table_schema:
Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)
Table: users (user_id PK, full_name VARCHAR, email VARCHAR, city VARCHAR, state VARCHAR)

description: Total order count by status (orders only)
question: How many orders are in each status?
plan_objective: From orders o, group by o.status. Return o.status and COUNT(o.order_id) as order_count ordered DESC.
table_schema: Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)

description: Average order value (AOV) by state (orders+users only)
question: What is the average order value per state?
plan_objective: Join orders o with users u on o.user_id = u.user_id. Group by u.state. Return u.state and AVG(o.grand_total) as avg_order_value ordered DESC.
table_schema:
Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)
Table: users (user_id PK, full_name VARCHAR, email VARCHAR, city VARCHAR, state VARCHAR)

description: Total revenue this month (orders only, DATE_TRUNC filter)
question: What is the total revenue this month?
plan_objective: From orders o, filter DATE_TRUNC('month', o.created_at) = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE). Return SUM(o.grand_total) as total_revenue.
table_schema: Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)

description: Total revenue by category — full 5-table chain, SUM(oi.total_price)
question: What is the total revenue by product category?
plan_objective: Join orders o -> order_items oi on o.order_id = oi.order_id -> product_variants pv on oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id -> products p on pv.product_id = p.product_id -> categories c on p.category_id = c.category_id. Group by c.name. Return c.name and SUM(oi.total_price) as total_revenue ordered DESC.
table_schema:
Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)
Table: order_items (item_id PK, order_id FK->orders, variant_id FK->product_variants, quantity INT, unit_price NUMERIC, total_price NUMERIC)
Table: product_variants (variant_id PK, product_id FK->products, sku VARCHAR, price NUMERIC)
Table: products (product_id PK, category_id FK->categories, name VARCHAR, status VARCHAR)
Table: categories (category_id PK, name VARCHAR, slug VARCHAR)

description: Total revenue by brand — full chain through brands, SUM(oi.total_price)
question: What is the total revenue by brand?
plan_objective: Join orders o -> order_items oi on o.order_id = oi.order_id -> product_variants pv on oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id -> products p on pv.product_id = p.product_id -> brands b on p.brand_id = b.brand_id. Group by b.name. Return b.name and SUM(oi.total_price) as total_revenue ordered DESC.
table_schema:
Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)
Table: order_items (item_id PK, order_id FK->orders, variant_id FK->product_variants, quantity INT, unit_price NUMERIC, total_price NUMERIC)
Table: product_variants (variant_id PK, product_id FK->products, sku VARCHAR, price NUMERIC)
Table: products (product_id PK, category_id FK->categories, brand_id FK->brands, name VARCHAR, status VARCHAR)
Table: brands (brand_id PK, name VARCHAR, country VARCHAR, segment VARCHAR)

description: Revenue by product (top 10) — SUM(oi.total_price), 4-table chain
question: What are the top 10 products by total revenue?
plan_objective: Join order_items oi -> product_variants pv on oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id -> products p on pv.product_id = p.product_id. Group by p.product_id, p.name. Return p.name and SUM(oi.total_price) as total_revenue ordered DESC. LIMIT 10.
table_schema:
Table: order_items (item_id PK, order_id FK->orders, variant_id FK->product_variants, quantity INT, unit_price NUMERIC, total_price NUMERIC)
Table: product_variants (variant_id PK, product_id FK->products, sku VARCHAR, price NUMERIC)
Table: products (product_id PK, category_id FK->categories, name VARCHAR, status VARCHAR)

description: Monthly revenue breakdown — SUM(oi.total_price) with DATE_TRUNC month grouping
question: Show monthly revenue for the last 6 months
plan_objective: Join orders o -> order_items oi on o.order_id = oi.order_id. Filter o.created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '6 months'. Group by DATE_TRUNC('month', o.created_at). Return the month and SUM(oi.total_price) as monthly_revenue ordered ASC by month.
table_schema:
Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)
Table: order_items (item_id PK, order_id FK->orders, variant_id FK->product_variants, quantity INT, unit_price NUMERIC, total_price NUMERIC)

description: Total revenue for Electronics category — full 5-table chain with WHERE filter
question: What is the total revenue from Electronics products?
plan_objective: Join orders o -> order_items oi on o.order_id = oi.order_id -> product_variants pv on oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id -> products p on pv.product_id = p.product_id -> categories c on p.category_id = c.category_id. Filter WHERE c.name = 'Electronics'. Return SUM(oi.total_price) as total_revenue.

description: Count distinct orders that included Clothing products

plan_objective: Join orders o -> order_items oi on o.order_id = oi.order_id -> product_variants pv on oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id -> products p on pv.product_id = p.product_id -> categories c on p.category_id = c.category_id. Filter WHERE c.name = 'Clothing'. Return COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) as order_count.

description: Revenue by city for Sports category — 6-table join with city group and category filter
question: What is the total revenue from Sports products by city?
plan_objective: Join orders o -> users u on o.user_id = u.user_id -> order_items oi on o.order_id = oi.order_id -> product_variants pv on oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id -> products p on pv.product_id = p.product_id -> categories c on p.category_id = c.category_id. Filter WHERE c.name = 'Sports'. Group by u.city. Return u.city and SUM(oi.total_price) as total_revenue ordered DESC.

question: Who are the top 5 customers by total spend?
plan_objective: Join orders o with users u on o.user_id = u.user_id. Group by u.user_id, u.full_name. Return u.full_name and SUM(o.grand_total) as total_spend ordered DESC. LIMIT 5.

question: Which 3 cities have the most orders?
plan_objective: Join orders o with users u on o.user_id = u.user_id. Group by u.city. Return u.city and COUNT(o.order_id) as order_count ordered DESC. LIMIT 3.

question: How many orders were placed in the last 30 days?
plan_objective: From orders o, filter o.created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'. Return COUNT(o.order_id) as order_count.
table_schema: Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)

description: Revenue this month — DATE_TRUNC('month', ...) = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
question: What is the total revenue this month?
plan_objective: From orders o, filter DATE_TRUNC('month', o.created_at) = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE). Return SUM(o.grand_total) as total_revenue.

description: Revenue in year 2024 — year boundary filter with >= and < pattern
question: What is the total revenue in 2024?
plan_objective: From orders o, filter o.created_at >= '2024-01-01' AND o.created_at < '2025-01-01'. Return SUM(o.grand_total) as total_revenue.
table_schema: Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)

description: Customers who bought Electronics but not Clothing — NOT EXISTS subquery
question: Which customers bought Electronics but never bought Clothing?
plan_objective: Select u.user_id, u.full_name from users u where EXISTS (subquery: join orders o -> order_items oi -> product_variants pv -> products p -> categories c where o.user_id = u.user_id and c.name = 'Electronics') AND NOT EXISTS (subquery: same chain where o.user_id = u.user_id and c.name = 'Clothing').
table_schema:
Table: users (user_id PK, full_name VARCHAR, email VARCHAR, city VARCHAR)
Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)
Table: order_items (item_id PK, order_id FK->orders, variant_id FK->product_variants, quantity INT, unit_price NUMERIC, total_price NUMERIC)
Table: product_variants (variant_id PK, product_id FK->products, sku VARCHAR, price NUMERIC)
Table: products (product_id PK, category_id FK->categories, name VARCHAR, status VARCHAR)
Table: categories (category_id PK, name VARCHAR, slug VARCHAR)

description: Products with no orders in the last 90 days — NOT EXISTS on order_items chain
question: Which products have had no orders in the last 90 days?
plan_objective: Select p.product_id, p.name from products p where NOT EXISTS (subquery: select 1 from order_items oi join product_variants pv on oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id join orders o on oi.order_id = o.order_id where pv.product_id = p.product_id and o.created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days').
table_schema:
Table: products (product_id PK, category_id FK->categories, name VARCHAR, status VARCHAR)
Table: product_variants (variant_id PK, product_id FK->products, sku VARCHAR, price NUMERIC)
Table: order_items (item_id PK, order_id FK->orders, variant_id FK->product_variants, quantity INT, unit_price NUMERIC, total_price NUMERIC)
Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)

description: Top-selling product in each category — RANK() OVER PARTITION BY in CTE with WHERE rank = 1
question: What is the top-selling product in each category?
plan_objective: Use a CTE: rank products by SUM(oi.total_price) within each category using RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY c.category_id ORDER BY SUM(oi.total_price) DESC). Join order_items oi -> product_variants pv -> products p -> categories c. Group by c.category_id, c.name, p.product_id, p.name. In outer query, select category, product and revenue where rank = 1.
table_schema:
Table: order_items (item_id PK, order_id FK->orders, variant_id FK->product_variants, quantity INT, unit_price NUMERIC, total_price NUMERIC)
Table: product_variants (variant_id PK, product_id FK->products, sku VARCHAR, price NUMERIC)
Table: products (product_id PK, category_id FK->categories, name VARCHAR, status VARCHAR)
Table: categories (category_id PK, name VARCHAR, slug VARCHAR)

description: Most popular payment method in each city — RANK() OVER PARTITION BY city
question: What is the most popular payment method in each city?
plan_objective: Use a CTE: rank payment methods by COUNT(*) within each city using RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY u.city ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC). Join orders o -> users u on o.user_id = u.user_id -> payments pay on o.order_id = pay.order_id. Group by u.city, pay.method. In outer query select city, method, cnt where rank = 1.
table_schema:
Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)
Table: users (user_id PK, full_name VARCHAR, email VARCHAR, city VARCHAR)
Table: payments (payment_id PK, order_id FK->orders, method VARCHAR, status VARCHAR, amount NUMERIC)

description: Customers spending above overall average order value — CTE for average, outer filter
question: Which customers have an average order value above the overall average?
plan_objective: CTE avg_overall: compute AVG(grand_total) from orders. CTE customer_avg: join orders o with users u on o.user_id = u.user_id, group by u.user_id, u.full_name, compute AVG(o.grand_total) as avg_spend. Outer query: select u.user_id, u.full_name, avg_spend from customer_avg cross join avg_overall where avg_spend > overall_avg ordered DESC.
table_schema:
Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)
Table: users (user_id PK, full_name VARCHAR, email VARCHAR, city VARCHAR)

description: Revenue comparison Q1 vs Q2 — two CTEs with non-overlapping windows
question: Compare total revenue in Q1 vs Q2 this year
plan_objective: CTE q1: SUM(grand_total) from orders where created_at >= '2024-01-01' AND created_at < '2024-04-01'. CTE q2: SUM(grand_total) from orders where created_at >= '2024-04-01' AND created_at < '2024-07-01'. Return both values side by side.
table_schema: Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)

description: Fix SUM(o.grand_total) to SUM(oi.total_price) when order_items is joined for category revenue
question: What is the total revenue by product category?
plan_objective: Join orders o -> order_items oi on o.order_id = oi.order_id -> product_variants pv on oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id -> products p on pv.product_id = p.product_id -> categories c on p.category_id = c.category_id. Group by c.name. Return c.name and SUM(oi.total_price) as total_revenue ordered DESC.

Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)

Table: products (product_id PK, category_id FK->categories, name VARCHAR, status VARCHAR)
Table: categories (category_id PK, name VARCHAR, slug VARCHAR)

validation_feedback.reason: SUM(o.grand_total) is incorrect when order_items is joined — grand_total repeats once per line item, inflating revenue counts
validation_feedback.repair_hint: Replace SUM(o.grand_total) with SUM(oi.total_price), which is the item-level revenue column and is not affected by row multiplication

description: Fix SUM(o.grand_total) to SUM(oi.total_price) in brand revenue query
question: Which brands generated the most revenue last month?
plan_objective: Join orders o -> order_items oi on o.order_id = oi.order_id -> product_variants pv on oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id -> products p on pv.product_id = p.product_id -> brands b on p.brand_id = b.brand_id. Filter o.created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'. Group by b.name. Return b.name and SUM(oi.total_price) as total_revenue ordered DESC. LIMIT 10.
table_schema:
Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)
Table: order_items (item_id PK, order_id FK->orders, variant_id FK->product_variants, quantity INT, unit_price NUMERIC, total_price NUMERIC)
Table: product_variants (variant_id PK, product_id FK->products, sku VARCHAR, price NUMERIC)
Table: products (product_id PK, category_id FK->categories, brand_id FK->brands, name VARCHAR, status VARCHAR)
Table: brands (brand_id PK, name VARCHAR, country VARCHAR, segment VARCHAR)

validation_feedback.reason: SUM(o.grand_total) inflates revenue when order_items is joined — grand_total is an order-level value that repeats for each item row
validation_feedback.repair_hint: Use SUM(oi.total_price) instead of SUM(o.grand_total) to correctly sum item-level revenue without row multiplication

description: Fix COUNT(o.order_id) without DISTINCT when order_items is joined
question: How many orders included Electronics products?
plan_objective: Join orders o -> order_items oi on o.order_id = oi.order_id -> product_variants pv on oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id -> products p on pv.product_id = p.product_id -> categories c on p.category_id = c.category_id. Filter WHERE c.name = 'Electronics'. Return COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) as order_count.
table_schema:
Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)
Table: order_items (item_id PK, order_id FK->orders, variant_id FK->product_variants, quantity INT, unit_price NUMERIC, total_price NUMERIC)
Table: product_variants (variant_id PK, product_id FK->products, sku VARCHAR, price NUMERIC)
Table: products (product_id PK, category_id FK->categories, name VARCHAR, status VARCHAR)
Table: categories (category_id PK, name VARCHAR, slug VARCHAR)

validation_feedback.reason: COUNT(o.order_id) without DISTINCT inflates the count when order_items is joined — each order_id appears once per line item
validation_feedback.repair_hint: Change COUNT(o.order_id) to COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) to count each order only once regardless of how many items it contains

description: Add missing product_variants bridge: order_items joined directly to products
question: What are the top 5 products by units sold?
plan_objective: Join order_items oi -> product_variants pv on oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id -> products p on pv.product_id = p.product_id. Group by p.product_id, p.name. Return p.name and SUM(oi.quantity) as units_sold ordered DESC. LIMIT 5.
table_schema:
Table: order_items (item_id PK, order_id FK->orders, variant_id FK->product_variants, quantity INT, unit_price NUMERIC, total_price NUMERIC)
Table: product_variants (variant_id PK, product_id FK->products, sku VARCHAR, price NUMERIC)
Table: products (product_id PK, category_id FK->categories, name VARCHAR, status VARCHAR)

validation_feedback.reason: order_items is joined directly to products on oi.product_id = p.product_id — this FK does not exist. order_items links to product_variants via variant_id, not directly to products.
validation_feedback.repair_hint: Add the product_variants bridge: JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id, then JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id. Remove the direct oi.product_id = p.product_id join.

description: Add missing order_items and product_variants: orders joined directly to categories
question: What is the revenue per category this month?
plan_objective: Join orders o -> order_items oi on o.order_id = oi.order_id -> product_variants pv on oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id -> products p on pv.product_id = p.product_id -> categories c on p.category_id = c.category_id. Filter current month. Group by c.name. Return c.name and SUM(oi.total_price) as total_revenue ordered DESC.
table_schema:
Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)
Table: order_items (item_id PK, order_id FK->orders, variant_id FK->product_variants, quantity INT, unit_price NUMERIC, total_price NUMERIC)
Table: product_variants (variant_id PK, product_id FK->products, sku VARCHAR, price NUMERIC)
Table: products (product_id PK, category_id FK->categories, name VARCHAR, status VARCHAR)
Table: categories (category_id PK, name VARCHAR, slug VARCHAR)

validation_feedback.reason: orders is joined directly to categories on o.category_id = c.category_id — this column and FK do not exist. The correct path goes through order_items, product_variants, and products.
validation_feedback.repair_hint: Replace the direct orders->categories join with the full chain: JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id, JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id, JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id, JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id. Also change SUM(o.grand_total) to SUM(oi.total_price).

description: Add missing product_variants bridge: order_items joined directly to brands
question: What is the total quantity sold per brand?
plan_objective: Join order_items oi -> product_variants pv on oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id -> products p on pv.product_id = p.product_id -> brands b on p.brand_id = b.brand_id. Group by b.name. Return b.name and SUM(oi.quantity) as total_units_sold ordered DESC.
table_schema:
Table: order_items (item_id PK, order_id FK->orders, variant_id FK->product_variants, quantity INT, unit_price NUMERIC, total_price NUMERIC)
Table: product_variants (variant_id PK, product_id FK->products, sku VARCHAR, price NUMERIC)
Table: products (product_id PK, category_id FK->categories, brand_id FK->brands, name VARCHAR, status VARCHAR)
Table: brands (brand_id PK, name VARCHAR, country VARCHAR, segment VARCHAR)

validation_feedback.reason: order_items is joined directly to brands on oi.brand_id = b.brand_id — this FK does not exist. The correct path goes through product_variants and products.
validation_feedback.repair_hint: Add the missing intermediate joins: JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id, JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id, then JOIN brands b ON p.brand_id = b.brand_id. Remove the invalid oi.brand_id join.

description: Fix invalid column p.product_name to p.name
question: What are the top 5 products by revenue?
plan_objective: Join order_items oi -> product_variants pv on oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id -> products p on pv.product_id = p.product_id. Group by p.product_id, p.name. Return p.name and SUM(oi.total_price) as revenue ordered DESC. LIMIT 5.
table_schema:
Table: order_items (item_id PK, order_id FK->orders, variant_id FK->product_variants, quantity INT, unit_price NUMERIC, total_price NUMERIC)
Table: product_variants (variant_id PK, product_id FK->products, sku VARCHAR, price NUMERIC)
Table: products (product_id PK, category_id FK->categories, name VARCHAR, base_price NUMERIC, status VARCHAR)

validation_feedback.reason: Column p.product_name does not exist in the products table. The correct column name for the product name is p.name.
validation_feedback.repair_hint: Replace p.product_name with p.name in both the SELECT clause and the GROUP BY clause. The products table uses 'name' not 'product_name'.

description: Fix invalid column u.username to u.full_name
question: Show the top 10 customers by total spend
plan_objective: Join orders o with users u on o.user_id = u.user_id. Group by u.user_id, u.full_name. Return u.full_name and SUM(o.grand_total) as total_spend ordered DESC. LIMIT 10.
table_schema:
Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)
Table: users (user_id PK, full_name VARCHAR, email VARCHAR, city VARCHAR, status VARCHAR)

validation_feedback.reason: Column u.username does not exist in the users table. The correct column for the customer's display name is u.full_name.
validation_feedback.repair_hint: Replace u.username with u.full_name in both the SELECT clause and the GROUP BY clause. The users table has 'full_name', not 'username'.

description: Fix invalid date column o.order_date to o.created_at
question: How many orders were placed in the last 30 days?
plan_objective: From orders o, filter o.created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'. Return COUNT(o.order_id) as order_count.
table_schema: Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)

validation_feedback.reason: Column o.order_date does not exist in the orders table. The correct timestamp column for order creation is o.created_at.
validation_feedback.repair_hint: Replace o.order_date with o.created_at in the WHERE clause. The orders table stores the order timestamp as 'created_at', not 'order_date'.
